options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: required_artifacts
options.description: How many Artifacts are required to finish

options.name: computer_sanity
options.description: If enabled, places checks on the Computer data locations

options.name: city_shuffle
options.description: Shuffles which doors lead to which cities

options.name: irresponsible_luigi
options.description: Causes Luigi to become irresponsible, able to talk to people from behind and cross the street without being on a sidewalk.

options.name: show_koopa_loot
options.description: If enabled, Koopas currently holding treasure will blink on the city map.

options.name: community_questions
options.description: Replaces all trivia questions in the game with randomly selected Archipelago trivia questions submitted by the community!

options.name: game_specific_questions
options.description:
If Community Questions is enabled, this option controls specific game-related questions.
None: All selected questions will only be relevant to Archipelago as a whole.
Only Relevant: Game specific questions can appear, but only for games currently being played in the multiworld. This option may select questions for 'Unsupported' games.
All: Game specific questions can appear, regardless of if that game is present in the multiworld or not. This will only pick questions from 'Supported' games.

options.name: shirt_color
options.description: Define a custom color for Luigi's green shirt. Color values are in HTML hex.

options.name: pants_color
options.description: Define a custom color for Luigi's blue pants. Color values are in HTML hex.

options.name: death_link
options.description: When you die, everyone who enabled death link dies. Of course, the reverse is true too.

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.
options: Spotlight from Christ the Redeemer Statue
Top Brick of the Great Pyramid

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.
options: Spotlight from Christ the Redeemer Statue
Top Brick of the Great Pyramid

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"
options: Spotlight from Christ the Redeemer Statue
Top Brick of the Great Pyramid

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.
options: Spotlight from Christ the Redeemer Statue
Top Brick of the Great Pyramid

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.
